creator: ae@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Aragon Gouveia from comment #0)
> When racoon has generate_policy enabled in a remote section, it successfully
> creates an SPD when an IPSec peer connects.  However at disconnect it fails
> to delete the SPD:
> 
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: INFO: deleting a generated policy.
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: get a src address from ID payload
> 192.168.7.166[59528] prefixlen=32 ul_proto=17
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: get dst address from ID payload 1.2.3.4[1701]
> prefixlen=32 ul_proto=17
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: sub:0x7fffffffdd10: 192.168.7.166/32[59528]
> 197.189.200.16/32[1701] proto=udp dir=in
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: db :0x801a7c310: 4.3.2.1/32[59528]
> 1.2.3.4/32[1701] proto=udp dir=in
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: sub:0x7fffffffdd10: 192.168.7.166/32[59528]
> 197.189.200.16/32[1701] proto=udp dir=in
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: db :0x801a7c490: 1.2.3.4/32[1701]
> 4.3.2.1/32[59528] proto=udp dir=out
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: call pfkey_send_spddelete
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: pfkey spddelete(inbound) sent.
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: call pfkey_send_spddelete
> 2017-08-28 23:51:56: DEBUG: pfkey spddelete(outbound) sent.

> key_spddelete: no SP found.
> 
> Between racoon and the kernel, I'm not sure who's getting the PF_KEY
> SPDDELETE call wrong.

I think this also may be related to NAT-T. It seems racoon uses not the same addresses in the SADB_SPDDELETE and the kernel fails to find corresponding policy. You can try to use 'setkey -x' to see what data racoon sends to the kernel, or just use more verbose logs. The addresses in SPIDX should match to address that you see in setkey -DP output.
